title: Jobshare - Security Engineer - Part time Jobshare
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** This is an exciting opportunity where we’re exploring local Security Engineering talent who need flexibility, with the potential to present qualified candidates to managers for part\-time or job\-share roles. Part\-time hours where two people share the responsibilities. JPMorgan Chase \& Co. (NYSE: JPM) is a top financial services firm with over $2 trillion in assets, operating in more than 60 countries. As a leader in investment banking and various financial services, the firm’s Cybersecurity \& Technology Controls (CTC) group partners with all business lines to enhance cybersecurity, access management, and controls. The CTC group focuses on enabling business operations while ensuring protection and resilience. This role is part of the CTC Product Security team, aligned with the Azure Cloud Enablement (ACE) program. As a Cloud Security Engineer at JPMorgan Chase within the Cybersecurity \& Technology Controls (CTC) group, your primary responsibility will be to ensure that Public Cloud is adopted in a secure and compliant manner. You will play an important role in identifying and managing risk related issues and actions with respective technology. You will have an eye for detail and an ability to see the big picture across security issues. **Job Responsibilities:** * Support the execution and enhancement of a long term information risk and control strategy designed to keep the information assets of the public cloud secure. * Deliver risk based assessments of secure technology controls relating to cloud services, cloud platforms and architectural components. * Support business technology teams to understand firm control requirements and implementations across a broad range of cloud architectures. * Perform security reviews of infrastructure\-as\-code for cloud platform development. * Contribute to documentation and agile processes in support of security programs. * Interface with wider CTC teams ensuring platform integration with security operations, threat intelligence, IAM and network security. **Required Qualifications:** * Formal training or certification on Information Security concepts and proficient applied experience * Eagerness to collaborate in a team, and comfortable in both virtual and office environments. * Self\-disciplined, self\-managed, self\-motivated and strong sense of ownership, urgency, and drive. * Proficient verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to effectively participate in discussions and meetings with internal management, peer groups, regulators and senior stakeholders. * Ability to prioritize and work under stringent timelines. **Preferred Skills:** * Keen desire to understand and secure public cloud technology. * AWS, Azure or Google Cloud certifications would be an advantage. * Hands on experience of developing, engineering or architecting within a public cloud environment would be an advantage. * Experience engineering with Terraform or infrastructure\-as\-code would be an advantage. * Understanding of DevOps or CI/CD concepts would be an advantage. Your Pathway to Jobsharing at JPMorgan – IT’S A 2 BRAINER! At JPMorgan we are passionate about supporting different ways of working to support our talent in the flexibility they need. We know that Jobshare is a fantastic way to hire talent for the firm that offers both the flexibility that you need whilst providing the consistency that our business requires. Jobshare is 2 people working part time hours with full time powers. This is an exciting opportunity where we’re exploring local Security Engineering talent who need flexibility, with the potential to present qualified candidates to managers for part\-time or job\-share roles. Part\-time hours; where two people share the responsibilities for one role. This is a **pipeline** role exploring **Jobshare o**pportunity for 19 hours per week. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success.

title: Join AECOM's Construction Project Management Talent Network
description: **Company Description** **Work with Us. Change the World.** At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations. There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world. We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us. **Job Description** **Start here. Grow here.** **Join Our Growing Construction Project Management Team at AECOM Ireland** At AECOM, we’re proud of our impressive and expanding Construction Project Management team delivering excellence across Ireland. As we continue to grow, we’re seeking talented, dedicated, and passionate professionals to join us and help shape the future of the built environment. If you have experience in the **Construction Project Management** sector and are looking to work on impactful, high\-profile projects in a dynamic and collaborative environment — we would be delighted to hear from you. **Ready to take the next step in your career?** Submit your CV today and become part of a global consultancy that values innovation, quality, and professional growth. **Why Join AECOM?** * **Impactful Projects:** Our construction project management team leads some of the largest commercial,healthcare,education,data center and infrastructure projects across Ireland. By joining us, you'll deliver designs that prioritise innovative and sustainable solutions, working on collaborative platforms and harnessing cutting\-edge technology. * **Professional Growth:** At AECOM, we prioritise the professional growth of our team members from day one. We offer fast\-track opportunities for development, making you a key member of a diverse, dynamic, and ever\-expanding team. * **Flexibility:** Given the varied and dynamic nature of our projects, we regularly require different skill sets and levels of experience. We're flexible and often create roles around specific skill sets to best suit our team and projects. * **Sector experience/workload** \- Commercial,healthcare,education,data center and infrastructure sectors * **Locations:** Dublin,Cork,Galway and Belfast If you have a construction project management background, please submit your CV against this role. Your application will be reviewed against all current and potential upcoming vacancies. **Who We're Looking For** We’re currently seeking experienced professionals to join our team in roles ranging from **Construction Project Manager** to **Associate Project Manager** . You’ll bring a strong background in construction project management, with proven experience delivering projects on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards. **Ideally, you will have experience in sectors such as:** * Healthcare * Education * Infrastructure * Data center * Residential **Come grow with us.** At AECOM, you’ll thrive in an inclusive and forward\-thinking environment where your contributions are valued \- and let's not forget the perks! You'll enjoy a range of core and personalised benefits designed to support your future and well\-being, which you can tailor to fit your lifestyle. Take advantage of career development opportunities, technical practice networks, AECOM University, volunteering days and our flexible hybrid working model to create a healthy work\-life balance. **Qualifications** **Ready to push the limits of what’s possible?** **Here’s What We’re Looking For** As a successful candidate, you will have experience working within our key markets — including **commercial** , **public sector** , and **infrastructure** projects. We are looking for individuals who combine strong technical expertise with excellent communication and leadership skills. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * A **postgraduate qualification** in Project Management, Construction, Engineering, Quantity Surveying, or a related field * Actively pursuing **Chartered Status** with one of the following professional institutions: * SCSI / RICS * CIOB * RIBA * Engineers Ireland (EI) * **APM affiliation** is considered an advantage You will thrive in a collaborative environment and be passionate about delivering high\-quality, client\-focused solutions across complex and high\-profile projects. We celebrate diversity, including neurodiversity, and believe it enriches our team. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and abilities. If you are an applicant with a disability that requires reasonable accommodation to complete the application process, please contact us at ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com. At AECOM, we value everyone's unique contributions and perspectives. If you meet some of the requirements above or have transferable skills you believe would benefit us, we would be delighted to hear from you! **Additional Information** Interested in the role or curious about life at AECOM? Follow us on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube to explore our AECOM voices, employee stories, latest projects, and much more! For further information about the role, reach out to the recruiter on LinkedIn Colum Calnan \\ **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. **ReqID:** REF44456H **Business Line:** B\&P \- Buildings \& Places **Business Group:** DCS **Strategic Business Unit:** Europe \& India **Career Area:** Construction **Work Location Model:** Hybrid

title: Junior Consultant - Training Provided
description: Are you an ambitious professional looking to make a significant impact in a new and dynamic field? If you have a passion for people and thrive in fast\-paced environments, we have an exciting opportunity for you! * **Unlimited Earning Potential: Fast\-Paced \& Rewarding Environment** * **Clear and Transparent Career Development – Progress to Leadership** * **Personalised Training – Use your Sales skills in a new exciting way** **What can you expect day to day?** As an Associate Recruitment Consultant, you'll have the freedom to do things differently, supported by colleagues with a wealth of experience, empowering you to learn and grow. No day is the same here at Access, and you will gain experience across a varied range of skills including: * Building relationships with clients and candidates * Managing vacancies from start to finish * Screening, interviewing and shortlisting candidates in the healthcare industry Here at Access, you really can influence your own earning potential, by getting out what you put in. Your energy and dedication will be rewarded in a culture built on celebrating success. **What we are looking for** : * 1 – 2 years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) – No previous recruitment experience is needed. * Passion to succeed and be part of a fast\-paced inclusive environment * Relationship\-focused with the confidence to build strong networks * Collaborative in spirit with a growth mindset to develop a long\-lasting career **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission (Average OTE: Year 1 €45k, Year 2 €75k) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: until 12 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making (all leaders grow inside) * Paid sabbatical * Wellness programme (e.g. time management, skills development, health talks, advise and activities, stress management) * Industry\-leading training programme with 200\+ training modules – Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and team socials If you are curious enough to know more, all you need to do is send us a current CV! francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie ***Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.***

title: Junior Consultant – Full training provided
description: * **Expert training from day one \- start strong, learn fast, and crush it from the jump.** * **Constant growth \- never settle, always level up, and keep your skills unstoppable.** * **Certified \& credible \- earn your stripes, stand out, and own your field.** Are you stuck in customer service or sales, hitting targets but seeing no real growth? Ready for a career where your results actually fuel your success. At **Access Healthcare** , we specialize in helping driven professionals from all kinds of backgrounds launch successful careers in recruitment. Our team is growing, and we’re looking for ambitious people ready to thrive in a fast\-paced, results\-driven environment and become Recruitment Consultants with us. **Why choose Access Healthcare for your career move?** * **From Zero to Expert:** 200\+ training modules, 1:1 coaching, and continuous development, you’ll never feel stuck. * **Continuous Development:** You’ll never feel stagnant. With nonstop training, you’ll keep developing your skills and growing your career. * **Get Certified:** Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant and build credibility that opens doors. **What You Will Need** * At least 2 year of experience in a sales\-focused role (e.g., Sales Rep, Sales Associate, Account Executive, Business Development, Inside sales, Tele sales representative, Sales Manager, Customer Success Manager, etc.) * An ambitious, growth\-driven mindset * A bachelor’s degree or equivalent is a plus but not required. **Total Compensation (Average Performer)** * Year 1: **€45,000\+** * Year 2: **€75,000\+** * Base salary: DOE * Commission: Uncapped, unlimited earning potential * Top performers earn significantly above these figures Ready for a new, exciting opportunity? Contact Maria de Gouveia at maria.degouveia@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+353 1 572 1202 to learn more about this thrilling opportunity. Take your career to new heights!

title: Junior Marketing Specialist
description: **Description** **About Sadie** Sadie is transforming how restaurants engage with their customers using AI\-powered voice technology. Our intelligent voice agent, Sadie, answers phone calls 24/7 to take bookings, manage reservations, answer FAQs, promote specials, and drive upsells—all without putting pressure on front\-of\-house staff. Sadie integrates with the world’s leading booking engines and POS systems to deliver a seamless guest experience and operational efficiency. We’re live in over 1,000 restaurants across Canada, the US, UK, Germany, and Australia, and we’re just getting started. **The Role** We are looking for a motivated and detail\-oriented Junior Marketing Specialist to support our growth across the UK and Ireland market. Based in Ireland, you will play a key role in executing Sadie’s marketing initiatives, helping drive brand awareness, customer engagement, and demand generation. This role is ideal for someone early in their marketing career who is eager to learn, experiment, and grow within a fast\-paced, high\-growth tech company. **What You’ll Do** * Support the development and execution of UK and Ireland\-focused marketing campaigns across digital, social, email, and events. * Assist in creating compelling content, including social posts, landing pages, newsletters, case studies, and sales collateral. * Monitor campaign performance and provide insights through regular reporting and analytics. * Collaborate with the sales and customer success teams to develop market\-specific materials and support outreach initiatives. * Conduct market research to identify trends, competitor activities, and opportunities for brand positioning. * Help manage Sadie’s presence at trade shows, webinars, and industry events. * Maintain and update Sadie’s website, marketing tools, and CRM entries as needed. * Contribute creative ideas to enhance marketing strategy and improve customer engagement. **What We’re Looking For** * 1–2 years of marketing experience (internships or co\-op experience welcome). * Strong communication and writing skills, with a keen eye for detail and storytelling. * Familiarity with social media platforms, digital marketing tools, and basic analytics. * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with shifting priorities. * Self\-starter with strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks. * Interest in hospitality, AI technology, or SaaS is a strong plus. * Experience with tools like HubSpot, Canva, Google Analytics, or similar platforms is an advantage. **Why You’ll Love Working at Sadie** * Join a fast\-growing startup that’s redefining how restaurants operate. * Work with a supportive, ambitious, and collaborative team across multiple markets. * Opportunities for rapid career growth and hands\-on learning. * Competitive compensation and benefits. * Flexible, remote\-friendly work environment. \#Sadie

title: Junior Mortgage Broker
description: We are hiring a **Mortgage Broker** for a Leading Mortgage Brokerage firm based in Dublin City Centre! This is an exceptional and rewarding opportunity for an ambitious Mortgage Advisor who is passionate about delivering unforgettable customer service and are eager to progress in their career. This is a **Hybrid Working Opportunity** offering great work \- life balance. **The Role:** * Help clients finance the purchase of properties and switch existing mortgages * Work within a team of advisors in a fast\-paced environment * Manage a portfolio of clients, providing advice to new clients and ongoing support throughout the mortgage completion process * Ensure accurate data entry at all times * Identify the best mortgage solutions * Manage and track related paperwork, including bank statements and lender applications * Liaise and negotiate with banks and lenders to secure the most suitable loan options for clients financial affordability **Ideal Candidate:** * At least 2\+ years' experience in providing mortgage advice in a bank or a brokerage firm * QFA (Qualified Financial Adviser) designation * Good communication skills and confident ability * Strong understanding of the full end to end mortgage process * Target\-driven with a proven track record of achieving goals **Benefits / Perks:** * Competitive Salary with Uncapped Commission * 25 Days Annual Leave * Pension Contribution * Death in Service * Income Protection * Hybrid Working Please apply through the portal OR you can send your CV to khickey@eirkoo.ie if you are interested in this opportunity. You can also phone (01\) 699 1200 and ask for Kate if you would prefer to have a confidential chat.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Dublin \- Dublin \- Ireland **About The Role** As a CBRE Project Management Analyst, you be responsible for providing administrative support to the project management team. This includes documentation preparation, escalation for commitment, financial modeling, financial reconciliations, and reporting. This job is part of the Project Management function. They are responsible for the management of projects from initiation through completion. **What You’ll Do** * Act as systems administrator of the internet\-based project tracking system, project websites and job cost accounting system. * Respond to internal and external customer inquiries concerning performance reports and project tracking system data. Escalate to management as needed. * Assist the team with the execution, monitoring and adherence of project administrative practices and policies. * Provide internal and external customer training on software applications. * Assist with close\-out procedures. * Train new administrative assistants and temporary employees on policies and administration. * Prepare routine and special client reports as required. * Have some knowledge of standard principles with limited practical experience in applying them. * Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values. * Impact the quality of own work. * Work within standardized procedures and practices to achieve objectives and meet deadlines. * Exchange straightforward information, ask questions, and check for understanding. **What You’ll Need:** * Bachelor's Degree preferred with up to 3 years of relevant experience. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered. * Ability to use existing procedures to solve standard problems. * Experience with analyzing information and standard practices to make judgments. * In\-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. title: Junior Software Developer
description: **Role Purpose** The Junior Software Developer will work closely with our application teams to develop, modify, and support our business applications \& systems. The software developer will be responsible for handling all aspects related to the provision and maintenance of software solutions in HRI. Reporting to the Business Applications Manager, the Junior Software Developer will assist in the development and maintenance of .NET applications such as Web Content Management System (CMS), RESTful API development and SharePoint development, working closely with senior developers to deliver high\-quality software solutions. This includes taking technical responsibility for maintaining \& supporting existing business applications \& systems, working on own initiative to find optimum solutions, in addition to ensuring that software systems are optimized for performance, and that the integrity and security is maintained to high standards, using industry best practice. **Key Responsibilities** * To keep up to date with best practice in Applications Development specifically Microsoft technologies using .Net with C\# as the development language and SQL Server as the database management system. * To apply a range of technical competence to all assigned work in terms of: * C\#, ASP.NET Technology stack (.NET CORE, MVC), Microsoft SQL Server, T\-SQL * ASP.NET Core web application development * Web Content Management System (CMS) application development * REST API development * SharePoint Development * To undertake specific application assignments and deliver these in accordance with the software development lifecycle project management best practice. * Provide support for complex \& critical business applications specifically the CMS web applications and SharePoint Sites and deal with issues title: Junior Software Engineer (FTC)
description: **PLAY, GROW and WIN** To be a part of Virtuos means to be a creator. At Virtuos, we harness the latest technologies to make games better and more immersive than ever before. That is why we pride ourselves in constantly pushing the boundaries of possibility since our founding in 2004\. Virtuosi is a team of experts – people who have come together to share their mutual passion for making and playing games. People with the same enthusiasm for exploring new ideas and the constant drive to excel in their field. People who believe in earning success through dedication. At Virtuos, we are at the forefront of gaming, creating exciting new experiences daily. Join us to Play, Grow and Win – together. **About The Position** Our studio is continuing to grow, and with new and exciting projects coming in, we are seeking a **Junior Software Engineer** for our studio located in Dublin, to join us in developing AAA games for some of the most renowned studios in the world. This position is ideal for people starting their career in games or have 1\-2 years of experience. **Responsibilities** * Implement and develop systems for NPC AI, UI, and general gameplay. * Deliver high\-performance, high\-quality, and well\-documented code. * Test and deploy your work on PC and consoles. * Build features for AAA games that run smoothly on a wide variety of consoles and platforms. * Use source control and bug reporting software. * Prioritize and execute tasks in a team environment. * Provide engineering support to our adjacent disciplines in art, animation, and design. **Qualifications** * Strong C\+\+ skills. * Experience with Unreal Engine or a Custom Engine and good knowledge of its gameplay and AI systems. * Competent at 3D mathematics related to gaming. * At least one solid example of implementing a game via work experience, game jam or college project. * Excellent problem\-solving, organizational, and communication skills. * Familiar with profiling and debugging tools. * Clear passion for gaming. **Desirable** * Professional experience working with AI and Gameplay in Unreal. * Agile development experience. * Tool creation experience. * Familiarity with other coding languages such as C\#, Python, Groovy. **About Us** Founded in 2004, Virtuos is one of the largest independent video game development companies. We are headquartered in Singapore with offices in Asia, Europe, and North America. Specializing in full\-cycle game development and art production, we have delivered high\-quality content for more than 1,500 console, PC, and mobile games. Our clients include 23 of the top 25 gaming companies worldwide. **About Our Team** Black Shamrock was founded in 2016 and acquired by the Virtuos Group in 2017\. The studio is Ireland’s largest PC and console game development studio and provides unique opportunities to work on multiple AAA titles. Our people are as diverse, exciting, creative, and dynamic as the projects that we work on. Black Shamrock is a place for talent to flourish and lifelong industry connections to begin. Our in\-house and full\-service capabilities include narrative, game, sound and level design, animation, art, rigging, engineering, and graphics, to name a few areas our skilled team members work on. At Black Shamrock, we take pride in helping put Dublin on the world map as the birthplace of award\-winning AAA games. **What We Offer** * Being part of one of the largest and fastest\-growing game development studios in Ireland * Dynamic career paths contributing to some of the world’s biggest AAA titles in gaming * Opportunities to grow in a tight\-knit community with a people\-oriented culture * Healthy work\-life balance and flexible work arrangements * Comprehensive company healthcare plan * Occupational Pension Scheme (including employer contribution) * Global training and development plans \- study support, formal education, international experience, career planning and progression opportunities * Bank for paid sick leave and wellness leave * Subsidised leave * Relocation support * Taxsaver plans * Career and international mobility opportunities with Virtuos Internal Mobility Program People matter. Diverse opinions and experiences matter. At Virtuos, our talented teams are the cornerstone of our success, and we recognize that fostering and advocating for inclusivity is at the center of what we do best \- we make games better, together. Virtuos is proud to be an equal opportunity employer that embraces diversity of thought, expression, culture, and backgrounds.

description: **Title: Senior Associate** **Department: Projects and Construction** William Fry is seeking a dedicated and ambitious senior lawyer to join our dynamic Projects \& Construction team at our Dublin office. This team provides expert advice on a wide range of construction, energy, infrastructure, and procurement matters. description: Role: Lawyer – Property Reporting to: Property Partner The successful candidate will be joining an innovative and market leading firm with a growing practice who will be involved in all aspects of Commercial Real Estate matters. title: Life Reserving Actuary
description: **Company Description** PartnerRe is a leading, privately owned, multi\-line global reinsurer with a reputation of financial stability and strength, and a commitment to rebuilding businesses and communities after risk events around the world. Our mission is to continue to be a financially stable and predictable business partner, supporting our clients with solutions tailored to their needs, delivering superior returns to our owner, providing rewarding opportunities for our people and making a positive contribution to society. To achieve this, we live by our values of Integrity, Performance, Collaboration, Straightforward Communication and Respect and Care. Core to The PartnerRe Experience is our global nature. No matter where you sit in the company, you are part of something bigger. Our teams represent our truly international mindset, forging connections across office locations and all levels of the organization and building a network of mutual success. The result is a day\-to\-day experience that will allow you to: **Shape your own career** **Grow in a supportive environment** **Connect with a community of experts** **Make an impact** View Home \- PartnerRe Experience e\-book to find out more about what it’s like to work at PartnerRe. **We are always looking for bright, proactive individuals to join our team!** Please follow our Careers page for all updates on new positions. We look forward to receiving your application. **PartnerRe is an equal opportunities employer.** Please join our company LinkedIn Page for all updates on new positions that are coming live. **Job Summary** We are seeking a Reserving Actuary based in Dublin and reporting to the Head of Actuarial Function. In this role you will support the Irish Legal Entity (PRESE) for our Life \& Health business. We are looking for someone who likes to work collaboratively across organizational and geographical boundaries, as well as independently and part of the team in a culture of continuous improvement. Your responsibilities will cover the independent review and oversight of the reserving process for PRESE, including GAAP, SII, PRA UK, and HK RBC reporting. **About The Role** * Review and analysis of the quarterly production liaising closely with the various stakeholders including the Central Risk Function, 1st Line Life Reserving team and External Reporting * Production of quarterly packs to the Reserving and Capital Committee and Board * Production of annual Actuarial Opinions on Solvency II Technical Provisions, Underwriting, Retrocession/Reinsurance and ORSA * Support to the Annual Assumption and Model change process * Support Internal, External audit and peer review activities * Inputs to various committees including the quarterly Life Data Quality Committee * Maintaining Actuarial Function documentation of key processes, procedures, and results * Supporting the continued enhancement of the SII Control Framework and DQ reporting * Review of new material transactions * Being a key contact for stakeholders located in Dublin including Finance, Risk and Operation teams * Supporting global projects and initiatives within Group Actuarial **Qualifications** **Technical Skills:** * You are a technically excellent individual with an ability to bring ideas to challenge existing approaches and develop new ones * Able to fit into dynamic structure (Multi GAAP environment involving new standards, tools and model modernization projects) * Knowledge of AXIS and R would be an advantage **Soft Skills** * You enjoy collaborating with your colleagues and working on your own. Your preference is to be part of an international and multicultural environment, handling diverse tasks, yet always ready to serve the team and meet the business needs * Strong communication skills and proficiency in English * Organized and able to work in a matrix structure * Able to set and manage priorities **Work Experience** * 5 years experience in the actuarial field, preferably in Life (re)insurance reserving or valuation * You may also have gained that experience at an actuarial consulting firm **Professional Qualifications** * Fellow or Associate of an internationally recognized actuarial society * Nearly qualified candidates will also be considered **Additional Information** PartnerRe provides a working environment in which diversity is valued, where all employees feel welcome, respected, and empowered to perform at their best. We invite you to learn more about D\&I at PartnerRe. Thank you, PartnerRe Hiring Team

title: MES Engineer
description: **bout Business Platforms:** Headquartered in Cork, Ireland, Business Platforms specialises in Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), Serialization, and Supply Chain Operations for our clients. We provide consulting services globally to the Pharmaceutical Industry in * Strategy Development and Execution * Business Process Analysis and Design * Change Management * Validation * Implementation and Support We partner with our clients to provide expertise and industry best practice to create and realize business value. **Role : MES Engineer** **Duration : Full time position** **Location : Dublin** **(Hybrid Working can be accommodated Client Site and WFH)** **Requirements of a Business Platforms MES Engineer :** * **3 \- 5 years’ experience** in GxP Manufacturing is required within the Pharmaceutical / Life Sciences Industries. * **3 \- 5 years’ experience** in MES projects involving MBR/Recipe design and qualification. * Demonstrate experience of aligning the client business process requirements with the MBR (Master Batch Records) technical design, participating in design review workshops, to ensure a successful project. * MBR/Recipe test protocol design and execution is required based on GxP regulations. * MES System Master data configuration and documentation. * Authoring and revision of applicable SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures). * Experience of training and supporting MBR/Recipe end users and improving site procedures. * Excellent interpersonal skills are required, strong verbal, digital and written communication skills * An enthusiastic, positive, and proactive attitude. * Team player, working with cross functional stakeholders, reporting to the client Project Manager (PM), ensuring milestone deliverables are met throughout. **Education :** * Hold a minimum of an Honours Level 8 degree in a Technical/Science/Engineering/ Business Information Systems discipline * Certification in Korber PAS/X or Rockwell PharmaSuite or Emerson Syncade is an advantage. **Remuneration \& Benefits :** * Business Platforms offers an extremely competitive base salary, commensurate with experience and skill\-set. * Great company benefits; medical and dental insurance, life assurance and pension. * Sports \& social events. * 23 days holidays p.a. * Business Platforms offers the opportunity for an accelerated career path with a holistic approach to lifelong learning, bespoke one\-on\-one training and support which will enable you to acquire new skills and develop. * At Business Platforms you will work as part of our Global MES Team, working with industry experts. You will be contributing to a variety of unique and challenging projects. * You can expect a positive culture, a collaborative and supportive environment where we encourage new ideas and innovation, to achieve the highest standard of outcomes for our clients.

title: Maintenance Fitter
description: Role Purpose The Maintenance Fitter is responsible for maintaining, troubleshooting, repairing, and improving manufacturing equipment and plant services to ensure maximum machine availability, efficiency, product quality, and safety. The role supports the smooth running of production by carrying out planned preventative maintenance, responding to breakdowns, and contributing to continuous improvement initiatives across the site. Key ResponsibilitiesMaintenance and Repairs * Carry out planned preventative maintenance on all production and facility equipment in line with the maintenance schedule. * Respond promptly to equipment breakdowns and carry out fault\-finding and repairs to minimise downtime. * Diagnose mechanical, pneumatic, and basic electrical faults on manufacturing and packing equipment. * Repair or replace worn and defective parts using appropriate tools and engineering techniques. * Assist with machine changeovers, line setups, and equipment adjustments as required. Equipment and Plant Reliability * Support the efficient operation of production lines, utilities, and site services. * Monitor machine performance and identify recurring issues or root causes of failure. * Recommend and implement improvements to enhance reliability, efficiency, and safety. * Participate in installation, commissioning, and validation of new machinery and equipment. * Ensure all work is completed to a high engineering standard and documented correctly. Preventative Maintenance * Complete daily, weekly, and monthly preventative maintenance tasks. * Maintain accurate maintenance records, work orders, parts usage, and downtime reporting. * Ensure spare parts are used responsibly and advise when stock replenishment is required. * Support the development and continuous improvement of the preventative maintenance programme. Compliance, Safety, and Hygiene * Carry out all duties in accordance with health and safety procedures, safe systems of work, and permit requirements. * Follow food safety, GMP, hygiene, and site housekeeping standards at all times. * Ensure maintenance activities do not compromise product safety, legality, or quality. * Adhere to lockout/tagout procedures and all engineering safety protocols. * Support audit readiness and compliance with site standards such as BRCGS, HACCP, and internal procedures. Teamwork and Communication * Work closely with production, quality, warehouse, and management teams to coordinate maintenance activities. * Communicate clearly on machine issues, repair status, and handovers between shifts where applicable. * Support operators with minor equipment issues and basic maintenance guidance where appropriate. * Assist in training colleagues on correct machine use and care where needed. Key RequirementsExperience * Previous experience as a Maintenance Fitter, Mechanical Technician, or Maintenance Engineer in a food manufacturing or FMCG environment. * Proven experience in mechanical maintenance, breakdown response, and preventative maintenance systems. * Experience working on production, processing, packing, or conveying equipment is desirable. * Experience in a regulated manufacturing environment with strong hygiene and food safety standards is preferred. Skills and Knowledge * Strong mechanical fitting, fault\-finding, and repair skills. * Good understanding of pneumatics, gearboxes, bearings, motors, conveyors, pumps, valves, and general production machinery. * Basic electrical fault\-finding ability would be an advantage. * Ability to read technical drawings, manuals, and maintenance instructions. * Good problem\-solving skills and a practical hands\-on approach. * Strong awareness of health and safety requirements in an industrial environment. * Good record\-keeping and computer literacy for maintenance systems and reporting. Qualifications * Trade qualified fitter / mechanical qualification or equivalent technical certification. * Relevant engineering apprenticeship preferred. * Manual handling, forklift, MEWP, or other technical certifications would be an advantage. Personal Attributes * Reliable, hands\-on, and proactive. * Able to work calmly under pressure and respond effectively to breakdown situations. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to high standards. * Good team player with strong communication skills. * Flexible and willing to support operational requirements. Pay: From €60\.00 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Medical Device Specialist – Batch Release
description: **Site Name:** Ireland \- Dublin **Posted Date:** Apr 10 2026 We are looking for a medical device specialist to join our team on a 12\-month fixed term contract basis. The successful candidate will prepare and review batch verification dossiers for medical devices supplied globally. You will work with manufacturing sites, external quality, supply chain and regulatory teams to ensure timely and compliant batch verification. We value people who are curious, dependable, and clear communicators. This role offers strong learning and growth opportunities and a chance to make a meaningful impact by helping ensure safe and effective products reach patients. Join us to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. This role is offered on a hybrid basis. You will work from the Dublin, Ireland site and from home as agreed with your manager. We create a place where people can grow, be their best, be safe, and feel welcome, valued and included. We offer a competitive salary, an annual bonus based on company performance, healthcare and wellbeing programmes, pension plan membership, and shares and savings programme. We embrace modern work practises; our Performance with Choice programme offers a hybrid working model, empowering you to find the optimal balance between remote and in\-office work. Discover more about our company wide benefits and life at GSK on our webpage *Life at GSK \| GSK* **In this role you will** * Prepare and review batch dossiers for medical device verification, including manufacturing, packaging and test records. * Ensure batches verified are maintained, and dossiers and samples archived in accordance with local SOPs and MDR 2017/745 requirements. * Key performance indicators generation and reporting. * SOP/report preparation and approval. * Raise, investigate and close deviations, support change controls related to batch verification. * Support periodic product reviews, complaints and adverse event reporting, and trending activities. * Maintain and support compliance with quality systems such as ISO 13485 and local regulatory requirements. * Communicate status and escalate quality issues to manufacturing sites, supply chain, external quality and regulatory stakeholders. * Participate in audits and self\-inspections and support responses to inspection findings. ***Why you?*** **Basic Qualifications \& Skills:** **We are looking for professionals with these required skills to achieve our goals:** * Minimum education requirement of Leaving Certificate (Higher Level) or equivalent, including QQI Levels 3–5 or Junior Certificate. A degree in a relevant scientific discipline is desirable. * Experience in quality review of batch records, deviations and test results within a regulated environment. * Working knowledge of medical device quality requirements, Good Manufacturing Practice or similar standards. * Experience with quality documentation, investigations, root cause analysis and corrective/preventive action processes. * Strong communication skills with experience working across multi\-disciplinary teams. * Proficient with common office IT tools and comfortable learning quality management systems. **Preferred Qualifications \& Skills:** **Please note the following skills are not necessary, just preferred, if you do not have them, please still apply:** * Familiarity with ISO 13485 and medical device regulatory requirements (for example MDR). * Experience with electronic quality systems such as Veeva QMS, Veeva Vault or similar. * Previous experience supporting regulatory inspections or presenting data to regulators. * Knowledge of distribution and storage quality practices. * Experience in batch release processes for products manufactured outside the region. * Experienced in manufacturing site operations, batch record review, quality data review and operational quality requirements * A detail\-focused approach with evidence of continuous improvement or process optimisation work. We manufacture and supply reliable, high\-quality medicines and vaccines to meet patients’ needs and drive our performance. Our network of 37 medicines and vaccines manufacturing sites delivered 1\.7 billion packs of medicines and 409 million vaccine doses in 2024 to help make a positive impact on the health of millions of people. Our supply chain is not just core to our operations; it’s vital to bringing our innovations to patients as quickly, efficiently and effectively as possible. Technology is transforming how we manufacture medicines and vaccines, enabling us to increase the speed, quality and scale of product supply. We need the very best minds and capability to help us on our journey to make more complex products, harnessing the power of smart manufacturing technologies including robotics, digital solutions and artificial intelligence to deliver for patients. **Closing Date for Applications – 24th April 2026 (COB)** Please take a copy of the Job Description, as this will not be available post closure of the advert. When applying for this role, please use the ‘cover letter’ of the online application or your CV to describe how you meet the competencies for this role, as outlined in the job requirements above. The information that you have provided in your cover letter and CV will be used to assess your application. **Why GSK?** **Uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together.** GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. We aim to positively impact the health of 2\.5 billion people by the end of the decade, as a successful, growing company where people can thrive. We get ahead of disease by preventing and treating it with innovation in specialty medicines and vaccines. We focus on four therapeutic areas: respiratory, immunology and inflammation; oncology; HIV; and infectious diseases – to impact health at scale. People and patients around the world count on the medicines and vaccines we make, so we’re committed to creating an environment where our people can thrive and focus on what matters most. title: Office Associate
description: **Office Coordinator** **Dublin** **Leading Investment Firm** **Permanent** **Starting ASAP** € **35\-45K \+ benefits** **7\-DubOC/LW** **The Client:** A fantastic opportunity to join one of the world’s leading private multi\-asset alternative investment firms. With multiple offices worldwide, their Dublin team (c.25 employees) is close\-knit, collaborative and welcoming. **About the Office Coordinator role:** This is an exciting opportunity for an efficient, high\-energy Office Coordinator to join a leading investment firm and play a key role in ensuring smooth office operations. This role will work across reception, office and facilities management, as well as assisting with company events. Responsibilities will include (but are not limited to): * Deliver seamless day\-to\-day office management, from guest reception to facilities liaison * Coordinate catering, refreshments, printing and postal needs * Provide support and diary management for one senior leader * Coordinate company events and off\-sites * Act as a key contact for the London office and HR team * Any additional ad\-hoc duties as required **Required Skills and Experience:** * *Previous office experience, preferably within financial or professional services* * Excellent communication skills and confident presence with senior stakeholders * Strong organisational skills with meticulous attention to detail * Proactive, solutions\-focused and adaptable to changing priorities * Tech savvy with Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint * Collaborative team player with positive energy and resilience **Additional Details:** * Permanent role * Salary c. €35\-45k plus excellent benefits * Based in Dublin, within a supportive and high\-performing team * A brilliant chance to grow your career in a prestigious global firm *If you have office experience and are looking to develop your skills in a dynamic environment, apply today!*

title: Orthopaedic Fellowship 2026
description: **Orthopaedic Fellowship 2026** **Hip \& Knee Arthroplasty** **Beacon Hospital, Sandyford, Dublin** **Hospital Overview** Located in South Dublin, Beacon Hospital is one of Europe's most advanced private hospitals. The hospital comprises: 181 inpatient beds, 70 day\-case beds, 8 operating theatres, 3 catheterisation labs and 4 endoscopy suites With over 1,600 healthcare professionals and more than 400 consultants, Beacon Hospital delivers 24\-hour, world\-class acute care across a wide range of specialties, including: Orthopaedics Cardiology Women's Health Urology Vascular Surgery ENT Neurology General Surgery Cancer Care General Medicine Cardiac Surgery Emergency Medicine **Post Details** * Department: Orthopaedic Department / UCD Beacon Hospital Academy * Commencement Date: 13 July 2026 Duration: 12 months **Reporting Structure:** * Clinically and professionally accountable to the supervising Orthopaedic Consultants * Administratively accountable to the Head of the NCHD Office (rostering, payroll, leave, etc.) **Fellowship Overview** Beacon Hospital is committed to delivering high\-quality patient care in a respectful, safe, and compassionate environment, in full compliance with all health and safety standards and regulations. This Fellowship offers a structured balance of: * 80% Clinical Practice 20% Research \& Teaching * **Key Features** * Experience in complex primary and revision hip and knee joint surgery * Exposure to sports orthopaedics, including management of sports\-related injuries * Participation in an established research programme, building on the work of Associate Professor Joseph M. Queally and Mr Khalid Merghani * A graduated responsibility model, preparing the Fellow on their path to independent specialist practice The Fellow will also benefit from access to: * UCD Beacon Hospital Academy Dedicated research support infrastructure under Beacon Hospital Research Institute * **Key Responsibilities** The Fellow will: Clinical Duties * Work under the supervision of Orthopaedic Consultants to theatre duties as assigned * Participate in operating theatre sessions, including primary and revision arthroplasty * When available attend and support ward rounds with Orthopaedic SHOs * Assess, document, and manage patient care in a timely and accurate manner * Support SHOs with patient admissions, reviews, and discharges Academic \& Teaching * Contribute to teaching medical students, junior doctors, and allied staff * Actively participate in research and ongoing academic development Professional Responsibilities * Maintain the highest standards of professionalism and patient care * Adhere to all hospital policies (e.g. JCI standards, Code Blue, infection control, working hours, etc) * Comply with health and safety regulations Support colleagues and provide guidance to more junior staff when required * **Person Specification** Qualifications * Full registration with the Irish Medical Council * Membership of the RCSI or equivalent (desirable) Experience * Minimum of one year's experience at Orthopaedic Registrar level in an acute hospital setting * Additional relevant training or subspecialty experience (desirable) **Job\-Specific Requirements** * Minimum IELTS score of 7 (for international applicants) Basic Life Support (BLS) certification * **Core Competencies** * Effective teamworking skills * Strong interpersonal and communication abilities Professionalism and organisational capability * **Additional Information** All roles at Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the efficient delivery of services. The successful candidate must be adaptable and responsive to the evolving needs of the hospital. Beacon Hospital is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Payroll Specialist
description: **Be part of something altogether life\-changing!** Working at Cytiva means being at the forefront of providing new solutions to transform human health. Our incredible customers undertake life\-saving activities ranging from fundamental biological research to developing innovative vaccines, new medicines, and cell and gene therapies. At Cytiva you will be able to continuously improve yourself and us – working on challenges that truly matter with people that care for each other, our customers, and their patients. With associates across 40\+ countries, Cytiva is a place where every day is a learning opportunity – so you can grow your career and expand your skills in the long term. Forming part of the Biotechnology segment at Danaher, we bring together dedicated technical expertise and talent to develop the next generation of life\-changing therapeutics. The UKI Payroll Specialist for Cytiva is responsible for the end\-to\-end processing of payrolls, ensuring timely and accurate payrolls in line with Ireland legislation and company policy. You’ll be responsible for maintaining precise payroll records, reconciling reports, submitting statutory declarations, and supporting compliance and internal audits. *If you’re a detail\-oriented professional with experience in Irish payroll, and a taste for problem\-solving, we want to hear from you!* This position is part of the *Payroll department* located in *Ireland* and will be *remote.* At Cytiva, our vision is, to advance future therapeutics from discovery to delivery. **What you will do:** * Deliver accurate and timely payroll every pay cycle; monitor vendor KPI and hold monthly review with payroll vendor, together with the payroll manager * Report any control issues on a timely basis to process owners * Strive for operational excellence: lookout for ideas to increase performance, maintain a customer service focus to resolve queries from associates, other HR functions and control partners as well as external parties such as local tax authority, payroll vendors, etc. * Define and document payroll related policies, practices and reporting * Support vendor transitions, consolidation or services **Who you are:** * 5\+ yrs experience in end\-to\-end Irish Payroll processing * Experience in analyzing processes and controls, planning improvements, leading changes, payroll migration and managing priorities. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills; excellent command of English read/write/speak * Result oriented, high on initiative with a need to deliver on time; willing to learn and assist others **Travel, Motor Vehicle Record \& Physical/Environment Requirements:** * Ability to travel internationally – 15% of the role **It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:** * *Payroll transitions and implementation* Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real\-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life. For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

title: Piping Designer
description: **About Tate** At Tate we are passionate about everything we do. As an independent brand operating within Kingspan Group, a global plc, Tate has been recognised worldwide as an industry leader in the development and manufacture of data centre infrastructure solutions and commercial office raised access floors for over 60 years. With revenues of over $500m and growing, Tate plays a pivotal role in offering expertise in cutting edge design engineering, working collaboratively with clients as a trusted partner. As a market leader within our industry Tate continues to grow and expand, operating 12 manufacturing sites across the US, Europe, and Australia. We are excited about our fresh, dynamic, and inclusive team of experts working on new innovations and forward\-thinking designs. We continue to invest heavily in the best available manufacturing tools and equipment needed to adhere to Tate's world class standards. In keeping with our Planet Passionate sustainability strategy, our mission is to accelerate a net zero emissions future built environment with the wellbeing of people and planet at its heart. **About the Role** The Piping Designer will lead the design and engineering of integrated piping systems, ensuring compliance with industry standards, and project specifications for data centre liquid cooling applications. You will work closely with the business development team to identify new opportunities, develop conceptual designs and drawings to be presented to potential customers. They will also work very closely with the Manufacturing team to ensure designs are optimal, practical, energy and cost efficient. **Key Responsibilities** **Piping Design and Layout**: Develop detailed piping designs, including 3D models, pdf drawings, and piping layouts for Data Centre projects **Stress Analysis**: Perform piping stress analysis ensure system integrity under various operating conditions. **Material Selection**: Specify piping materials, components, and fittings based on project requirements **Compliance and Standards**: Ensure designs adhere to industry and client specifications. **Project Coordination**: Collaborate with project managers, commercial team, and other stakeholders to integrate piping systems with overall project designs. **Technical Leadership**: Mentor and guide junior engineers, review their work, and provide technical direction. **Documentation**: Prepare and review piping specifications, bill of materials (BOM), and technical reports. **Site Support**: Provide technical support during construction, commissioning, and troubleshooting of piping systems. **Cost Management:** Contribute to project cost estimation and ensure designs are completed within budget and timeline. **Qualifications, Experience \& Skills** **Education:** HNC / Degree / Post Graduate Degree Qualifications in Mechanical / Building Services Engineering. **Experience:** 5\+ years of industry experience in piping design and engineering in construction, pharmaceuticals, semiconductor or oil \& gas industries Exposure to Data Centres or Critical environments is highly desirable. **Skills** * Good working knowledge of manufacturing processes, materials, and quality control protocols. * Proficiency in 3D modelling and piping design software (e.g. Solidworks, Revit, AutoCAD, PDMS, SP3D, CADWorx). * Expertise in stress analysis tools * Knowledge of industry codes and standards * Experience developing new products and taking them from concept to prototype to production. * Strong communication and teamwork skills. * Excellent time management and organisational skills, with the ability to set objectives and prioritise workloads for yourself. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and interface with sales, customer service, purchasing, manufacturing, engineering, quality and marketing. Stay connected with us on *LinkedIn* for insights into life at Tate. Tate is an equal\-opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Join us in our mission to make a difference through exceptional solutions.

title: Platform Engineer
description: **About the role** As a Platform Engineer, you will play a key role in ensuring the reliability, scalability, and performance of Prism products and supporting infrastructure in Azure. This is a hands\-on role where you’ll implement and optimize platform services, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment processes for enterprise\-grade applications. The Platform Engineer should be a can\-do, high\-achieving individual with strong product and domain expertise, who thrives on collaboration and has experience building secure, resilient cloud infrastructure. You’ll champion engineering excellence and automation, foster best practices, and bring a genuine passion for technology and continuous improvement to everything you do. **Location:** Dublin, Ireland. *Permanent, Hybrid working model.* **Your responsibilities** * Development and maintenance of Azure infrastructure using best tools and practices. * Design and configuration of CI/CD pipelines, containers, virtual networks, and monitoring dashboards. * Design and implementation of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) scripts for multi\-tenant setups. * Performance monitoring and optimization of Azure resources, including cost and performance advice. * Implementation of governance policies: Azure AD, security, virtual networks, etc. * Collaboration with development, test, and Scrum teams to ensure quality and timely delivery. **Your skills** * 4\+ years of experience in delivering fast paced and agile system Engineering ideally in regulated FinTech or payments/banking/wealth. * Excellent knowledge of Azure Cloud, Azure AD, and security in Azure. * Proficient in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) practices and tools (Terraform). * Strong advocate of CI/CD methodologies, familiar with the Azure DevOps stack. * Deep understanding of cloud artefacts/tools: containers, API management, event bus, app services, monitoring, etc. * Standardized logging/metrics/tracing (OpenTelemetry, Prometheus/Grafana); built comprehensive dashboards and runbooks. * Proven track record of building high\-performing and reliable infrastructures. **Success Metrics (what you will be measured on)** * DORA: Lead time, Deployment frequency, Change failure rate, MTTR. * Reliability: SLO attainment (%), incident rate and severity mix, mean time to detect (MTTD). * Quality: Escaped defects, defect density, automated test coverage, flaky test rate. * Security/Compliance: Vulnerability SLA adherence, audit/control pass rate. * Cost \& Efficiency: Cloud unit economics, cost\-to\-serve, build time, onboarding time to first PR. **Pepper Advantage’s values and culture** Our mission is to help people succeed. Our clients, our customers, our employees. Our Values are ‘Can do’, ‘Balanced’ and ‘Real’. Pepper Advantage’s values support our vision, shape our culture and define how we interact with other employees and the attitudes we adopt towards our customers and clients. The values of Pepper Advantage support the organisation’s mission statement. Pepper Advantage’s unique outcome focused corporate culture aims to deliver fairness and value for clients and customers, consistently exceeding expectations in all measurable performance areas. *Pepper Advantage is an equal opportunities employer.* *Role profiles are subject to change in line with business needs.*

title: Podiatrist - Nursing Home/ Clinic Cover

We would love to hear from you! **What is the role and what are your responsibilities?** * Treat a variety of skin ailment such as verruca’s, corns, calluses, etc. * Assess and treat ingrown toenails, clipping toe nails and general feet tidy up * Treat patients with diabetes at increased risk of foot disease who require preventative care and assistance * Determine the condition of a patient’s feet, ankles, or lower legs by reviewing his or her medical history, listening to the patient’s concerns, and performing a physical examination * Diagnose foot, ankle, and lower\-leg problems through physical exams, and other methods * Provide treatment for foot, ankle, and lower leg ailments, such as prescribing special shoe inserts (orthotics) to improve and correct foot and ankle deformities * Give advice and instruction on foot and ankle care and on general wellness techniques * Educate patients on how to improve and/or improve conditions * Liaise with other healthcare professionals, such as GP’s, Physios and other members of multi\-disciplinary teams, to exchange information about the background and progress of patients and Refer patients to other physicians or specialists if they detect larger health problems, such as diabetes * Meet continuous education requirements and maintain an up to date CPD folder * Write patient case notes and reports and collect statistics; as well as managing their payments, and issue receipts * Place stock orders and log repairs * In some cases, supervise student and junior podiatrists and enhance your teaching skills * Manage clinical risk and have excellent infection control awareness * Be caring, compassionate and professional and always have the patient at the core of everything you do * Help with creating blogs and content for social media and other marketing channels * Work with the marketing team and provide them with on the ground feedback from time to time **What experience and qualifications do I need?** * BSc OR MSc in Podiatry (essential) * CORU registration (essential) * Member of the Society of Chiropodists and Podiatrists Ireland (SCPI) (essential) * Professional Indemnity Insurance (essential) * Private practice experience (preferred) * New grads welcome **If you have any queries about the role, please contact 01 290 2690** Please note that if you click "apply" you consent to your information to be stored for 12 months for recruitment purposes by Spectrum Health.
